response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend web developer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js profile, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which he does not have, constituting a hard disqualifier on its own. Beyond that, he lacks the core technical competencies required: embedded networking knowledge (L2/L3, MACsec, 802.1X), deep C programming for UNIX/Linux embedded systems, Linux kernel modification experience, and any demonstrable background in network security or switch firmware development.
strengths: Over a decade of broad software development experience across multiple languages and platforms, including some C/C++ work at National Instruments
Demonstrated ability to work in embedded and hardware-adjacent contexts (robotic arms, NI LabWindows/CVI, hardware control at Altom)
Strong engineering discipline with TDD, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship practices
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role
No relevant security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption, attack/tamper detection) anywhere in the resume
No embedded networking or Linux kernel development experience; career is predominantly web and application-layer software

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
